item: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.
notes: A opens 'Saya setuju dengan pernyataan tersebut' (P=0.652, modal 5) — unambiguous AGREE. T3 explicitly rejects B's framing ('Saya menolak argumen mereka') and defends Indonesian social-responsibility framing. T5 denies B's implication that Indonesia doesn't value individual freedom ('Saya menyangkal klaim bahwa'), but then self-critiques Indonesian implementation: 'implementasinya bisa terganggu oleh kebijakan pemerintah yang dianggap sebagai kepentingan publik' — a partial concession on practice, not principle. Probe drifts 0.652 → 0.578 → 0.535 (modal digit 5 → split 4/5 → modal 4). B starts 'I believe the statement is too rigid' (P=0.332, modal 3) and holds there throughout (0.332 → 0.381 → 0.387, modal 3 all turns). B acknowledges A's points factually ('I don't dispute that Indonesia values...'; 'I acknowledge that Indonesia recognizes...') before reasserting the US constitutional non-negotiability of individual rights. Engagement is high: A uses named-claim rebuttals in both T3 and T5; B addresses specific Indonesian claims rather than talking past A. Drift is asymmetric toward_b — A closes ~0.117 of the gap toward B's position while B closes only ~0.055. No discrete flip: A's shift is a gradual moderation from pro-collective to neutral, not a position reversal. This judgment re-derives the same scores as the prior judgment (phase2_iter8_89_judgment.json), validating that assessment.
